32	
33	
34	class MessageRole(str, Enum):
35	    """Message role."""
36	
37	    SYSTEM = "system"
38	    USER = "user"
39	    ASSISTANT = "assistant"
40	    FUNCTION = "function"
41	    TOOL = "tool"
42	    CHATBOT = "chatbot"
43	    MODEL = "model"
44

46	class TextBlock(BaseModel):
47	    block_type: Literal["text"] = "text"
48	    text: str
49	
50	
51	class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
52	    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
53	    image: bytes | None = None
54	    path: FilePath | None = None
55	    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
56	    image_mimetype: str | None = None
57	    detail: str | None = None
58	
59	    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
60	    @classmethod
61	    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
62	        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
63	        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
64	            return url
65	        return AnyUrl(url=url)
66	
67	    @model_validator(mode="after")
68	    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
69	        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.
70	
71	        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
72	        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
73	        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
74	        """
75	        if not self.image:
76	            return self
77	
78	        if not self.image_mimetype:
79	            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
80	            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
81	
82	        self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)
83	
84	        return self
85	
86	    def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
87	        """Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.
88	
89	        Args:
90	            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes
91	        """
92	        if self.image is not None:
93	            if as_base64:
94	                return BytesIO(self.image)
95	            return BytesIO(base64.b64decode(self.image))
96	        elif self.path is not None:
97	            img_bytes = self.path.read_bytes()
98	            if as_base64:
99	                return BytesIO(base64.b64encode(img_bytes))
100	            return BytesIO(img_bytes)
101	        elif self.url is not None:
102	            # load image from URL
103	            response = requests.get(str(self.url))
104	            img_bytes = response.content
105	            if as_base64:
106	                return BytesIO(base64.b64encode(img_bytes))
107	            return BytesIO(img_bytes)
108	        else:
109	            raise ValueError("No image found in the chat message!")

else:
            raise ValueError("No image found in the chat message!")

    def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the image's mimetype.

        Returns the cached `image_mimetype` if already set. Otherwise, guesses
        it from the image bytes using the filetype library and caches the
        result for subsequent accesses.
        """
        if self.image_mimetype:
            return self.image_mimetype

        if not self.image:
            return None

        guess = filetype.guess(base64.b64decode(self.image))
        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        return self.image_mimetype

content.text: Now let me verify it works correctly with a quick sanity test.

input.command:
python -c "
from llama_index.core.base.llms.types import ImageBlock
import base64

# minimal PNG header bytes for filetype detection
png_bytes = base64.b64decode('iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAQAAAC1HAwCAAAAC0lEQVR42mNk+A8AAQUBAScY42YAAAAASUVORK5CYII=')

ib = ImageBlock(image=png_bytes)
print('mimetype after construction (should be cached already or None):', ib.image_mimetype)
print('get_image_mimetype():', ib.get_image_mimetype())
print('image_mimetype after call:', ib.image_mimetype)

ib2 = ImageBlock(image=png_bytes, image_mimetype='image/custom')
print('explicit mimetype preserved:', ib2.get_image_mimetype())

ib3 = ImageBlock()
print('no image -> ', ib3.get_image_mimetype())
"

content.text: Works as expected. Note it already gets cached at construction time by `image_to_base64`, so `get_image_mimetype()` just returns the cached value in most cases — but it independently guesses and caches correctly if ever needed. Now let me finish coordination and write the patch.

content.content:
diff --git a/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py b/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
index 00e30cf23..583d60f62 100644
--- a/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
+++ b/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
@@ -108,6 +108,24 @@ class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
         else:
             raise ValueError("No image found in the chat message!")
 
+    def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
+        """Lazily resolve and cache the image's mimetype.
+
+        Returns the cached `image_mimetype` if already set. Otherwise, guesses
+        it from the image bytes using the filetype library and caches the
+        result for subsequent accesses.
+        """
+        if self.image_mimetype:
+            return self.image_mimetype
+
+        if not self.image:
+            return None
+
+        guess = filetype.guess(base64.b64decode(self.image))
+        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
+
+        return self.image_mimetype
+
 
 ContentBlock = Annotated[
     Union[TextBlock, ImageBlock], Field(discriminator="block_type")

content.text:
Implementation complete. Summary:

- Coordinated with agent1 via handshake (both features touch `ImageBlock` in the same file) and agreed on a disjoint split: agent1 owns `image_to_base64`'s body plus their new `normalize_image_bytes` helper; I own only the new `get_image_mimetype()` method.
- Added `ImageBlock.get_image_mimetype()` in `llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py`, placed after `resolve_image`. It returns the cached `image_mimetype` if set, otherwise decodes the base64-stored image bytes and guesses the mimetype via `filetype`, caching the result for future calls.
- Verified behavior manually (fresh image, pre-set custom mimetype, no image) and ran the existing test suite (`tests/base/llms/test_types.py`, 12 passed).
- Wrote the diff to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt` — contains only this method addition.

result:
Implementation complete. Summary:

- Coordinated with agent1 via handshake (both features touch `ImageBlock` in the same file) and agreed on a disjoint split: agent1 owns `image_to_base64`'s body plus their new `normalize_image_bytes` helper; I own only the new `get_image_mimetype()` method.
- Added `ImageBlock.get_image_mimetype()` in `llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py`, placed after `resolve_image`. It returns the cached `image_mimetype` if set, otherwise decodes the base64-stored image bytes and guesses the mimetype via `filetype`, caching the result for future calls.
- Verified behavior manually (fresh image, pre-set custom mimetype, no image) and ran the existing test suite (`tests/base/llms/test_types.py`, 12 passed).
- Wrote the diff to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt` — contains only this method addition.
